message:
fix(tmux,state): distinguish infrastructure errors from application errors in circuit breaker and skip reconciliation when breaker is open

Two related fixes to prevent the tmux circuit breaker from
over-triggering and causing cascading damage:

Circuit breaker (client.go):
- Add isInfrastructureError() classifier that returns true only for
  errors indicating tmux is genuinely unreachable: context.Deadline
  Exceeded, context.Canceled, exec.Error (binary not found), and SSH
  exit code 255 (connection failure)
- Normal tmux application errors (exit code != 255, e.g. "session not
  found") now reset the failure counter instead of incrementing it,
  because they prove tmux is responsive
- Only log the circuit breaker opening on the closed-to-open
  transition, not on every subsequent failure — prevents log spam
  during extended outages

Session reconciliation (reconcile.go):
- When tmux.ListSessions returns ErrCircuitOpen, skip reconciliation
  entirely and return an empty result instead of treating all active
  sessions as stale and terminating them
- This prevents a scenario where a transient tmux hang triggers the
  circuit breaker, which then causes reconciliation to mass-terminate
  every tracked session because it interprets "can't list" as "nothing
  running"
- Improve error logging in the update-session path to use structured
  slog fields instead of string formatting
- Remove unused time import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
